fragility_points: A sharp BTC selloff over the next five days could erase the current cushion.
Any Binance-specific anomaly at the exact resolution minute would matter disproportionately.
key_assumptions: Current Binance spot and recent 1-minute candles fairly represent current venue state.
The contract resolves exactly from the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute candle close on April 20.
Five-day downside and venue-specific resolution risk are small but not negligible.
main_logical_chain: The contract settles on a precise Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute close on April 20.
Current Binance spot is materially above 68000, making Yes the clear base case.
But a single-minute, single-venue five-day-ahead crypto contract still carries nonzero downside and operational tail risk.
Therefore Yes should be favored strongly, but somewhat less strongly than the 97.15% market price implies.
main_thesis: Yes remains the base case because Binance BTC/USDT is well above 68000, but the market is somewhat overconfident for a one-minute, one-venue resolution five days ahead.
quote_anchors: Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone
source_quality_view: Primary source quality is high for contract mechanics and current venue pricing; contextual consensus evidence is useful but not highly independent.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC is already comfortably above the strike, so No still needs a meaningful downside move or a venue-specific edge case.
The market's bullish consensus is supported by a real spot cushion, not pure narrative.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT was around 74613 during the run, roughly 9.7% above the 68000 strike.
Recent Binance 1-minute klines were tightly clustered around 74.6k.
Polymarket ladder pricing showed uncertainty concentrated higher up the ladder, not near 68k.
timing_relevance: High: the contract resolves at noon ET on April 20 from one specific 1-minute Binance candle, so date/time mechanics are central.
unresolved_ambiguities: How much short-horizon volatility should be charged against the current cushion.
Whether Binance interface-displayed candle data and API data are perfectly aligned at the resolution minute.
what_would_change_view: I would move closer to market if BTC stays firmly above 74k into April 19-20 and nearby strikes also compress toward near-certainty; I would move lower if BTC weakens materially or Binance-specific source-risk rises.
